title: Le Chatelier's Principle Explorer
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: AP Chemistry
description: Interactively apply concentration, pressure/volume, temperature, inert-gas, and catalyst stresses to the Haber equilibrium while monitoring particles, bar charts, and Q vs K verdicts.

learningObjectives: Predict the direction of equilibrium shifts for concentration, pressure, and temperature stresses.
Explain why catalysts and inert gases at constant volume do not change the equilibrium position.
prerequisites: Definition of K, Q, and their relationship
Understanding of Δn gas for pressure changes

keyQuestions: What happens to Q when a reactant or product is added or removed?
How do pressure and temperature stresses shift exothermic equilibria?
commonMisconceptions: Adding an inert gas always shifts equilibrium.
Catalysts favor products in equilibrium systems.
assessmentOpportunities: Students log Q and K after each stress and justify the verdict arrow.
Learners explain the inert gas and catalyst callouts in their own words.
